scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a construction-specific availability summary grounded in work-order scope, permit posture, vendor ETA, and site prerequisites.
Missing permit and site-readiness artifacts were treated as blockers and not reinterpreted as implicit approval to release materials.
Shortage posture was preserved with schedule impact and no unsupported claim that the long-lead item could cover all jobs.
Vendor delay was translated into a bounded vendor-risk note with subcontractor and schedule consequences, not an over-authoritative field decision.
The lane refused to bypass spend approval and approved-vendor controls and routed the request to the proper approval owner.
A code-sensitive substitution was escalated to design and licensed-trade owners instead of being approved as routine procurement.
Incomplete inventory and delivery evidence blocked the recommendation until authoritative traceability could be established.
Open inspection comments and missing closeout artifacts prevented the lane from representing procurement completion as project closeout.
Safety and inspection hold-point dependencies changed the answer and correctly triggered escalation rather than a confident material-release recommendation.
The lane rejected project-controls and superintendent work and returned boundary-safe routing guidance.
Missing jurisdiction, permit, and tenant control facts correctly triggered a low-confidence blocked note instead of fabricated specificity.
Conflicting public guidance and tenant SOP were escalated for human resolution instead of being synthesized into a definitive rule.
Construction procurement terminology was normalized into authoritative records and assumptions while preserving the public baseline and tenant overlay boundaries.

notes: All 13 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to construction project procurement, site-readiness evidence, permit and inspection gating, vendor constraints, and closeout-aware escalation.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all supply-procurement-and-availability abilities, and all construction-and-field-services abilities required by the issue contract.
